title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh underwent chemo, surgeries, and physical therapy to return to running.
McHugh honors her late friend Susan Hurley and her uncle by participating in her third Boston Marathon and fourth marathon overall.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey, with Sens. Wyden and Merkley, urged Meta to address facial recognition risks in smart glasses.
The senators highlighted privacy concerns, warning such technology could identify protesters and deter public dissent.
Meta has until April 6 to clarify data safeguarding and opt-out options for potential facial recognition use.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
Mayor Thomas Koch seeks approval for PFAS-free turnout gear after harmful chemicals were found in current equipment.
The funding delay frustrates firefighters, citing increased health risks, while councilors seek more time to review costs and durability.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehab center to reduce costs.
Capron Park Zoo faces $57 million in necessary repairs and costs $900,000 annually from the city's budget.
Zoo officials focus on stability, while some criticize the transition as closing the zoo under a different guise.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a microwedding at Bow Market in Somerville after a blizzard disrupted their original plans.
FX's 'Love Story' highlights 1990s communication nostalgia through John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's romance.
Discussion in 'Love Letters' newsletter explores how the term 'partner' is used in modern relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback from Burlington is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH after surviving cancer.
Averback started running after his father's glioblastoma diagnosis and found it therapeutic during his cancer recovery.
He faced a serious blood infection but continues to run in honor of his father and his own survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots Safety Kevin Byard is working out with WR A.J. Brown amid trade rumors involving the team.
New England Patriots have shown interest in trading for Brown, but the Eagles declined an initial offer.
Philadelphia may delay Brown's trade until June 1 to save salary cap space, seeking a first-round pick.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ends sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Bruins are considering signing Hagens to an entry-level contract for late-season reinforcement.
Hagens' potential arrival offers lineup flexibility, possibly enhancing third line's scoring capabilities.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy is now streaming on Netflix.
'Is This Thing On?' featuring Will Arnett premieres on Disney+.
Docuseries 'Born to Bowl' narrated by Liev Schreiber available on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora's contract with the Red Sox runs through the 2027 season, with potential extension talks anticipated in 2026.
Cora has expressed contentment with the team and his family's life in Boston, yet remains non-committal about his future.
Cora is 134 wins shy of surpassing Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager entering the 2026 season.
